pubmed_links: [Shaheen et al. (2013) Novel syndrome of hypohidrosis and intellectual disability linked to COG6 deficiency](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23606727/); [Xia et al. (2022) Swedish COG6-CDG experience and comprehensive literature review](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36636598/); [Cirnigliaro et al. (2022) COG6-CDG: Novel variants and novel malformation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35068072/)
summary: COG6, encoding a subunit of the Conserved Oligomeric Golgi complex, is linked to two overlapping neurodevelopmental conditions: Shaheen syndrome (intellectual disability, hypohidrosis, microcephaly) caused by a hypomorphic intronic variant, and COG6-CDG, a congenital disorder of glycosylation featuring intellectual disability, ADHD, corpus callosum agenesis, delayed myelination, and progressive microcephaly. (emerging evidence)
